meta.title: PRSLOY — private access by invite
meta.description: Closed beta access to a private internet channel. Personal configuration, manual issuing, human support.
meta.ogDescription: Closed beta. Personal configuration. Access by request.

hero.sub_features: INFRASTRUCTURE CHOSEN BY REPUTATION, NOT PRICE

globe.label: INFRASTRUCTURE
globe.subtitle: We choose the servers ourselves — by reputation, not by who is cheapest. That is why the connection stays steady: it does not drop, and it does not crawl.
globe.hint_desktop: DRAG TO ROTATE · HOVER A POINT
globe.hint_touch: DRAG TO ROTATE · TAP A POINT

nothing.label: YOUR CONNECTION · CLOSED BETA
nothing.meta_route: Configuration is issued by a person, not a bot.
nothing.meta_invisible: OWN ROUTE · REAL SUPPORT · NOTHING EXTRA

pricing.label: PRICE
pricing.guarantee: Email — for the invite only · No newsletters · Support is a real person
pricing.access_note: Invite, configuration and guide arrive after a capacity check

faq.label: QUESTIONS
faq.q1: What does this actually run on?
faq.a1: On servers we choose by the provider’s reputation, not the price tag. Cheap infrastructure lands on blocklists and under sanctions — and takes everyone’s access down with it. We don’t go there.
faq.a2: It keeps capacity and quality in our hands. Opening access in small pools beats onboarding more people than the infrastructure can comfortably carry.
faq.a3: Access works through a configuration and a short guide in your dashboard. Our own client is in the works — but access shouldn’t be hostage to a single app.

pricing_page.label: PRICING
pricing_page.subheadline: Personal configuration. Manual issue. No extra setup.
pricing_page.waitlist_description: Leave your email — we check available capacity and send the next step. Public checkout stays closed for now: quality first, scale later.
pricing_page.success_description: We will send the next step when capacity is available for new access. No newsletters.
pricing_page.error_description: Try again in a minute or message us on Telegram.
pricing_page.error_invalid_email: That looks invalid — check and resend.
pricing_page.step_1_body: Leave your email. We see the request and check whether there is capacity for new access.
pricing_page.step_2_body: If the pool is open, we send the next step and attach access to your PRSLOY ID.
pricing_page.step_3_body: Once issued, your dashboard shows a personal configuration and a short setup guide.
pricing_page.beta_note: No public checkout yet: access is issued manually during private beta

setup_page.label: SETUP
setup_page.title: Setup without extra steps
setup_page.subtitle: Once access is issued, the dashboard shows your personal configuration. This public page explains the flow: what to prepare, where to take the link, and what to do if the client rejects it.

checklist.title: Access is issued
checklist.body: Your dashboard should show an active status and a personal configuration. If it does not, the account is still under manual review.

checklist.title: One account
checklist.body: Use the same email you requested the invite with. It keeps review history and access issuing tied to one PRSLOY ID.

checklist.title: Private link
checklist.body: Do not share the configuration with other people. It is tied to your account and can be replaced manually.

setup_page.happ_title: Happ — a compatible client for every device
setup_page.happ_body: Happ is the compatible client we recommend for importing your personal configuration. It runs on phones, computers and TV devices and reads the configuration as a subscription link.

platforms.label: iOS / Android
steps: Install Happ from the official App Store or Google Play listing.
Open the dashboard and copy your personal configuration.
In Happ, add it as a subscription link and turn the profile on.

platforms.label: Windows / macOS / Linux
steps: Install the desktop Happ build for your operating system.
Paste the copied configuration into the import field.
Refresh the profile list if the active state does not appear right away.

platforms.label: Android TV / Apple TV
steps: Install Happ on the TV device or set-top box.
Move the configuration to the device with the clipboard or a QR code.
Import it as a subscription and keep the configuration private.

setup_page.security_body: We do not publish universal setup details on the landing page. The link is created for an account and shown only in the dashboard, so it is easier to replace, revoke, or inspect manually.

steps.title: Copy the configuration
steps.body: In the dashboard, press Copy in the personal configuration block. If the link is hidden, press Show first.

steps.title: Open a compatible client
steps.body: Import the configuration as a subscription link or paste it into a manual input field. Exact labels depend on the client.

steps.title: Check the active state
steps.body: After import, the client should show an active profile and should not ask for a new key. If the state does not change, refresh the profile list.

steps.title: Keep access private
steps.body: Do not publish the link or send it to shared chats. If the link leaked or stopped working, contact support.

setup_page.troubleshooting_body: If the client rejects the configuration, update the app, copy the link from the dashboard again, and make sure you use the same email. If the error remains, message us on Telegram with a screenshot.
setup_page.no_key_body: Request an invite first and verify your email. Once capacity is approved manually, the personal configuration appears in the dashboard.

dashboard_page.label: DASHBOARD
dashboard_page.title: My access
dashboard_page.subtitle: Only the useful parts: access status, personal configuration, setup guide, and support.
dashboard_page.feature_status_body: Whether your key is active, valid until when, how much traffic is left (if there’s a cap).
dashboard_page.feature_renew_body: Pay for a new period without regenerating the key. The route is preserved, devices stay connected.
dashboard_page.feature_regen_body: If the key leaked or broke — issue a new one in one tap. The old one is automatically revoked.
dashboard_page.feature_config_body: Link or QR code for adding it to a new client. All your devices run off the same key.
dashboard_page.feature_support_body: Direct chat with an engineer. Not a bot, not autoresponders — a real person who sees your key and the infrastructure.
dashboard_page.feature_history_body: Payments and renewals list. No traffic telemetry — billing only.
dashboard_page.web_soon_body: Once manual issuing is stable, we will add more features directly in the browser dashboard — no Telegram detour.
dashboard_page.setup_body: Email login is built into the site, but the service is not fully configured in production yet.
dashboard_page.auth_required_body: Sign in or create a PRSLOY ID to see your access and setup instructions.
dashboard_page.verify_body: Verify your email so support can safely attach an invite and access key to this account.
dashboard_page.verify_error: Could not send the email. Try again in a minute.
dashboard_page.console_body_ready: Your account is active. Keep the configuration private and use the setup guide when adding a new device.
dashboard_page.console_body_pending: Your identity is in the queue. Email verification and capacity review happen first; the configuration appears here after manual issue.
dashboard_page.privacy_body: No public config links and no fake checkout state. The dashboard only shows what is attached to this account.
dashboard_page.status_ready_body: Your configuration is attached to this account. Copy the link, open the guide, and add the profile to Happ or a compatible client.
dashboard_page.status_pending_body: The account is created. Once access is issued, the personal configuration and setup buttons will appear here.
dashboard_page.status_blocked_body: Contact support with your account email. We will check the status and tell you what to do next.
dashboard_page.key_ready_body: This is your private link. Do not post it in shared chats and use it only on your own devices.
dashboard_page.key_pending_body: The key has not been issued yet. There is nothing to configure now: once access is ready, this card will show the link and actions.
dashboard_page.copy_error: Could not copy. Select the link manually.
dashboard_page.ready_hint_label: Access is issued. Keep the link private and use it only in your own client.
dashboard_page.pending_hint_label: The account is ready for manual access attachment. Once capacity is approved, your personal configuration appears here.
dashboard_page.next_active_1_body: Open the setup guide, choose your device, and import your personal link into the client.
dashboard_page.next_active_2_body: After connecting, make sure the client shows an active state and does not ask for a new key.
dashboard_page.next_active_3_body: If setup fails, send your account email and an error screenshot. We will handle it manually.
dashboard_page.next_pending_1_body: We can see the account and will attach access manually after review. If it is urgent, contact support.
dashboard_page.next_pending_2_body: One email equals one PRSLOY ID. It keeps the invite and issue history easy to track.
dashboard_page.next_pending_3_body: If the invite is urgent, contact support with your account email.
dashboard_page.reissue_body: Use this if the link leaked or stopped working. After replacement, the old access should no longer be used.
dashboard_page.reissue_sent_body: Request sent. Support will replace the configuration manually.
dashboard_page.reissue_error_body: Could not send the request. Try again in a minute.
dashboard_page.reissue_rate_limited: Too many requests. Try again later.
dashboard_page.reissue_no_key: There is no active configuration to reissue yet.
dashboard_page.reissue_auth_required: Session expired. Refresh the page and sign in again.
dashboard_page.support_body: If setup fails, send your account email and an error screenshot. No bots, no long forms.

auth.login_subtitle: Use the email and password you created on the site. Telegram is no longer required for web access.
auth.register_subtitle: Create a site account. We’ll verify the email and attach access here when the invite is issued.
auth.invalid: Use a valid email and a password with at least 8 characters.
auth.email_exists: This email already has an account. Sign in instead.
auth.not_configured: Account storage is not connected yet.
auth.storage_not_configured: Account storage is temporarily unavailable. Please try again shortly.
auth.secret_not_configured: The account service is not fully configured yet. Please try again later.
auth.rate_limited: Too many attempts. Wait a couple of minutes and try again.
auth.generic_error: Could not complete the request. Try again in a minute.

faq_page.label: FAQ
faq_page.title: What you need to know
faq_page.subtitle: Direct answers without public technical instructions.

items.a: An invite-only private access service. Once issued, you get a personal configuration and a setup guide in your dashboard.

items.q: Why invite-only and not for everyone?
items.a: Because quality matters more than growth. We open access in small pools so infrastructure stays calm and support can help each user.
For now access works through a compatible configuration and dashboard guide. Our own client is in development, but access should not depend on a single app.

items.q: How is the connection set up?
items.a: We do not publish technical schemes publicly. For the user it is simple: account, personal configuration, and a guide after access is issued.
We watch route quality and help switch if a specific configuration becomes unstable.

items.q: How many devices can I use?
items.a: The beta is meant for personal devices of one user. For family or team access, message support first.

items.q: How do I get an invite?
items.a: Leave your email on the pricing page. If capacity is available, we send the next step and attach access to your PRSLOY ID.
Public checkout is not open yet. During beta, access is issued manually: request first, capacity check, then the next step by email.
It is the beta access reference price so positioning is clear. Automatic checkout comes only after manual issuing is stable.
The minimum needed for account and access issuing: email, verification status, access status, and the technical identifier of the issued configuration.
No private access service deserves blind trust. Our honest stance: no magic promises, no unnecessary public schemes, and closed beta until quality is reliable.

privacy.label: PRIVACY
privacy.title: What we know about you
privacy.intro: This is not a corporate privacy policy with 40 clauses. Here’s an honest list of what we collect, why, and how long we keep it.

sections.p: Only what’s needed for the service to work: email, verification status, access status, the identifier of your access in our system, subscription start and end date. That’s it.

Activity logs — which sites you visit, what you look up, who you connect to. Payment data (cards, account numbers) — these go directly to the payment gateway. Biometrics, contacts, geolocation. There are no email newsletters or marketing trackers on the site.

Public payment is not open right now. During private beta, access is issued manually after request and capacity check. If a paid step appears, terms and the payment processor will be shown before payment.

Your key record lives while the subscription is active + 90 days (in case of renewal). After that — deleted. Email from the waitlist form is kept until we send you the invite link, then deleted within 30 days if you don’t subscribe.
Email service for confirmations and notifications, hosting for the site and infrastructure, Telegram if you message support. We do not share your activity history with third parties.
You can request deletion of any of your data — message support from the email tied to your PRSLOY ID. After identity verification, we delete within 7 days.
If we change something — we update the date at the top and send a product notice when the change matters. We don’t slip in material changes silently.

terms.label: TERMS
terms.title: Rules of use
terms.intro: Short rules. This is not an enterprise SLA — these are beta-service terms written so they can actually be read.

sections.p: A personal configuration for private access, access validity, and a dashboard guide. During private beta, issuing is manual so we can control quality.

sections.h: WHAT YOU CAN USE IT FOR
sections.p: Personal secure connectivity, protecting data on public networks, and access to your work and personal resources. Publicly distributing or reselling the key is not allowed.

sections.h: WHAT YOU CAN’T USE IT FOR
sections.p: Spam and DDoS. Attacks on infrastructure (ours or anyone else’s). Distributing harmful software. Automated data collection above reasonable limits (thousands of requests per second). The operator reserves the right to disable the key without refund in case of abuse — but we’ll ask first, then disable.

The service is in closed beta. We do everything we can to keep routes up 24/7, but we can’t guarantee 100% uptime — that would be a lie. If a node falls, the route switches; if something stays down for a long time, we compensate by extending the subscription.
Public payment is closed for now. If access is issued through an individual paid step, payment and refund terms are fixed before payment. If something goes wrong, message support.

You can stop renewing at any time — just don’t pay. We can stop servicing the key in case of these terms being violated — after notifying you through the account contact. If we terminate without your fault — we refund the unused portion of the payment.
We provide a technical private access service. How you use the connection is your responsibility. The service does not make you anonymous and is not a security guarantee by itself.

refunds.label: REFUNDS
refunds.title: Refund and cancellation policy
refunds.intro: Clear rules for private beta access. If the service does not work for you, contact support and we will handle the request without loops or hidden steps.

sections.p: For the first paid access period on a PRSLOY account, you may request a refund within 14 calendar days from payment if the service quality is not acceptable for you.

The first purchase from a unique account is eligible for review. Renewals, repeated purchases, upgrades, and extra periods are not automatically refundable because they confirm that the previous access period was accepted.

A refund can be refused if access was materially consumed, if available traffic accounting shows more than 5 GB of total usage after activation, or if the account was blocked for abuse, spam, attacks, malware, resale, or other violations of the Terms.

If there is a connection issue, contact support first. We may provide setup steps, a replacement configuration, another route, or an extension of the access period. If we cannot make the service usable for you, the refund request stays eligible within the refund window.

There is no mandatory long-term commitment. If recurring payments are enabled, you can cancel future renewals before the next billing period through the available account controls or by contacting support.
Message support at https://t.me/prsloy. Include the email tied to your PRSLOY ID, payment date and amount, a receipt or payment screenshot, and a short reason for the request.
We review refund requests within 7 business days. After approval, the payment processor or payment method may need additional time to return the funds.
